mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.
description: Phase 2: Wire model resolution logic into the live loop engine. Each iteration resolves the correct model via synergy cluster analysis, passes --model to Claude subprocess, injects escalation policy for non-opus models, auto-escalates on crash recovery, and displays the active model in iteration headers.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks
criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

userStories.title: Initial tests for spawn_claude --model flag
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for the --model flag on spawn_claude(). Use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo approach to verify argument ordering. Tests should initially fail or use #[ignore].
acceptanceCriteria: Test: when model is Some('claude-opus-4-6'), echoed args contain '--model claude-opus-4-6' before '-p'
Test: when model is None, echoed args do NOT contain '--model'
Test: when model is Some(''), treated as None — no --model flag in args
Known-bad discriminator: test that --model appears BEFORE -p (rejects implementations that append --model after -p)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail)
userStories.notes: TDD: Write these tests first. Use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo to capture args. The spawn_claude function currently has no model param — tests define the expected new signature.
edgeCases: Empty string model Some('') must be treated as None — no --model '' passed to subprocess
Model string with spaces or special characters: should pass through verbatim
Very long model string: should not truncate
invariants: --model flag must always appear BEFORE -p flag when present
When model is None, arg list must be identical to current behavior
--print and --dangerously-skip-permissions must always be present regardless of model

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test fails — flag position matters for CLI parsing
Claude CLI may error on empty model string

correctness: Tests must verify exact arg ordering, not just presence
Empty string must be normalized to None
style: Use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo pattern consistent with existing tests in claude.rs

userStories.title: Initial tests for prompt model resolution
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for model resolution in build_prompt(). Tests should verify PromptResult.resolved_model is populated correctly based on task model, difficulty, prd_default, and synergyWith relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: task with explicit model='claude-opus-4-6' → resolved_model is Some('claude-opus-4-6')
Test: task with difficulty='high' and no explicit model → resolved_model is opus
Test: task with no model, no difficulty, prd_default='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' → resolved_model is haiku
Test: task with synergyWith partner that has higher-tier model → resolved_model is the higher tier
Test: no default_model in prd_metadata → resolved_model falls back to None gracefully
Known-bad discriminator: synergy partner with opus model overrides selected task's haiku → rejects implementations that ignore synergy
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail)
userStories.notes: TDD: Tests define expected behavior for PromptResult.resolved_model and BuildPromptParams.default_model. The synergy cluster uses explicit synergyWith relationships from the task_relationships table, NOT file overlap. Use insert_relationship() from test_utils.rs to set up synergyWith relationships in the test DB.
edgeCases: No default_model in prd_metadata → falls back to None
Task with empty string model Some('') → normalized to None in resolved_model
No synergyWith partners → synergy cluster is just the selected task
Multiple synergy partners with different tiers → highest tier wins
invariants: Tier precedence: task_model > difficulty='high' > prd_default > None
Synergy cluster model = max tier across all cluster members

failureModes.cause: prd_metadata table has no default_model column (pre-v7 DB)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Skip missing synergy partner, don't panic

correctness: Must test all tier precedence combinations
Synergy escalation must pick highest tier not first found
style: Use test helpers consistent with existing prompt.rs tests

userStories.title: Initial tests for escalation template loading
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for load_escalation_template() and its integration into build_prompt(). Tests should verify template is loaded for non-opus models and omitted for opus.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: load_escalation_template with valid file returns Some(content)
Test: load_escalation_template with missing file returns None
Test: escalation section injected into prompt when resolved_model is haiku or sonnet
Test: escalation section NOT injected when resolved_model is opus or None
Known-bad discriminator: test that template content appears BEFORE reorder instruction (rejects implementations that append template at end)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail)
userStories.notes: TDD: Template path resolved from base_prompt_path's parent directory (e.g., scripts/prompt.md → scripts/escalation-policy.md). Use temp directories for test isolation.
edgeCases: Missing template file → None returned, warning printed to stderr
Empty template file → Some('') returned, empty section injected (harmless)
Template file with unicode content → passed through verbatim
invariants: Template is loaded fresh each iteration (not cached) to allow hot-editing
Escalation section is NEVER present when model is opus or None
Escalation section appears BEFORE reorder instruction in prompt ordering

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Return None, print warning to stderr
Resolve relative to current directory or return None gracefully

correctness: Template presence/absence must correlate exactly with model tier
Section ordering in prompt must match PRD spec
performance: File I/O per iteration is acceptable — template is small
style: Use tempdir for test isolation, consistent with existing prompt tests

userStories.title: Initial tests for crash recovery model escalation
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for crash recovery escalation in IterationContext. Tests verify that same-task crashes trigger model escalation and different-task iterations do not.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: same task_id + last_was_crash=true → escalate_model() called, model goes up one tier
Test: different task_id + last_was_crash=true → no escalation
Test: same task_id + last_was_crash=false → no escalation
Test: escalation from haiku → sonnet, sonnet → opus verified
Test: already opus + crash → stays opus (no panic, no error)
Test: resolved_model=None + crash → stays None (can't escalate unknown baseline)
Known-bad discriminator: test that escalation only triggers when BOTH conditions met (same task AND crash)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail)
userStories.notes: TDD: Tests define expected IterationContext fields (last_task_id, last_was_crash) and the escalation check logic. Escalation is independent of CrashTracker backoff — they operate separately.
edgeCases: First iteration: last_task_id=None → no escalation possible
Crash on task with None model → stays None
Multiple consecutive crashes on same task → escalates once per crash (haiku→sonnet→opus→opus)
invariants: Escalation requires BOTH same task_id AND last_was_crash
Escalation ceiling is opus — never goes beyond
None model is never escalated (can't escalate unknown baseline)
Escalation is independent of CrashTracker backoff logic

failureModes.cause: last_task_id not set after iteration completes
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Next iteration has no crash context, no escalation

correctness: Both conditions (same task + crash) must be checked
Escalation uses model::escalate_model() — don't reimplement
style: Test crash scenarios without spawning real processes

userStories.title: Initial tests for iteration header model display
userStories.description: Write tests BEFORE implementation for model display in print_iteration_header(). Tests verify the model name appears in the header output.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: model=Some('claude-sonnet-4-6') → header contains 'Model: claude-sonnet-4-6'
Test: model=None → header contains 'Model: (default)'
Test: header still contains iteration count, task_id, and elapsed time alongside model
Known-bad discriminator: test that model display is present in the formatted output (rejects implementations that accept the param but don't render it)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] or expected to fail)
userStories.notes: TDD: print_iteration_header currently outputs to stderr via eprintln!. Tests may need to capture stderr or refactor to a format function. Consider adding a format_iteration_header that returns String for testability, with print_iteration_header as a thin wrapper.
edgeCases: Very long model name → should not break formatting (truncate_display may apply)
Model name with unicode characters → display verbatim
Model name that is literal string 'None' → displays as 'Model: None' not 'Model: (default)'
invariants: Iteration count, task_id, and elapsed time always present regardless of model
Model display always present — either name or '(default)'

failureModes.cause: Model param added but not rendered in output

correctness: Model text must be visually present in header output
style: Consistent with existing display.rs formatting patterns

userStories.title: Add --model flag to spawn_claude subprocess (US-009)
userStories.description: Add model: Option<&str> parameter to spawn_claude(). When Some(m) and m is non-empty, insert --model and m into args before -p. When None or Some(''), omit --model entirely. Update the sole call site in engine.rs to pass None for backward compatibility.
acceptanceCriteria: spawn_claude() signature is (prompt, signal_flag, working_dir, model: Option<&str>)
When model is Some('claude-opus-4-6'), args are: --print --dangerously-skip-permissions --model claude-opus-4-6 -p <prompt>
When model is None, args are unchanged: --print --dangerously-skip-permissions -p <prompt>
When model is Some(''), treated as None — no --model flag
Sole call site in engine.rs updated to pass None
All TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
cargo check and cargo clippy pass with no warnings
userStories.notes: Guard empty strings: if let Some(m) = model { if !m.trim().is_empty() { cmd.args(['--model', m]); } }. The call site in engine.rs (~line 349) passes None initially — FEAT-006 will wire the real model later.

correctness: --model must appear before -p in arg list
Empty string must be guarded
Existing behavior unchanged when None
performance: No overhead when model is None
style: Use if let Some(m) pattern, not unwrap()
Guard with !m.trim().is_empty()

userStories.title: Add model display to iteration header (US-012)
userStories.description: Add model: Option<&str> parameter to print_iteration_header(). Display 'Model: <name>' when Some, 'Model: (default)' when None. Update sole call site in engine.rs to pass None initially.
acceptanceCriteria: print_iteration_header() signature adds model: Option<&str> as last parameter
Header output includes 'Model: claude-sonnet-4-6' when model is Some
Header output includes 'Model: (default)' when model is None
Existing header info (iteration, task_id, elapsed) unchanged
Sole call site in engine.rs (~line 344) updated to pass None
All TEST-INIT-005 tests pass
userStories.notes: Consider adding a format_iteration_header() -> String for testability, with print_iteration_header as a thin eprintln! wrapper. The call site passes None initially — FEAT-006 wires the real model.

correctness: Model display must always be present — either name or '(default)'
Existing header elements unaffected
style: Match existing eprintln! formatting style in display.rs
Use truncate_display for long model names if needed

userStories.title: Prompt builder model resolution (US-008)
userStories.description: Wire model resolution into build_prompt(). Add default_model to PrdMetadata and BuildPromptParams. Add resolved_model to PromptResult. Query synergyWith partners' model/difficulty, resolve task models, pick highest tier for iteration. Normalize Some('') to None.
acceptanceCriteria: PrdMetadata struct in engine.rs has default_model: Option<String> field
read_prd_metadata() queries default_model from prd_metadata table
BuildPromptParams has default_model: Option<&'a str> field
PromptResult has resolved_model: Option<String> field
New helper load_synergy_partner_models(conn, task_id) queries synergyWith partners' model and difficulty from task_relationships table (columns: related_id, rel_type)
build_prompt() calls resolve_task_model for selected task and each synergy partner
build_prompt() calls resolve_iteration_model with all resolved models
resolved_model is normalized: Some('') becomes None
When no synergyWith partners exist, cluster is just the selected task
When prd_metadata has no default_model, falls back to None gracefully
run_loop passes default_model through run_iteration to BuildPromptParams
All TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.notes: Synergy cluster uses explicit synergyWith relationships from task_relationships table (NOT file overlap — deliberate simplification from PRD FR-006). Query pattern: SELECT t.model, t.difficulty FROM tasks t INNER JOIN task_relationships tr ON tr.related_id = t.id WHERE tr.task_id = ? AND tr.rel_type = 'synergyWith' AND t.status IN ('todo', 'in_progress'). Thread default_model: read_prd_metadata → run_loop → run_iteration → BuildPromptParams → build_prompt. Use insert_relationship() from test_utils.rs for test setup.

correctness: Tier precedence: task_model > difficulty=high > prd_default > None
Synergy cluster uses highest tier across all members
Some('') normalized to None
performance: Single DB query for synergy partners' model data
style: New helpers are pure where possible
DB queries use parameterized statements
Use existing model.rs functions — don't reimplement tier logic

userStories.title: Escalation policy template injection (US-010)
userStories.description: Create the escalation-policy.md template file. Add load_escalation_template() helper that resolves the template from base_prompt_path's parent directory. In build_prompt(), inject the escalation section when resolved_model is non-opus (tier < Opus). Section goes BEFORE reorder instruction.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/escalation-policy.md created with content from PRD appendix
New helper load_escalation_template(base_prompt_path: &Path) -> Option<String>
Template path resolved as base_prompt_path.parent()/escalation-policy.md

When resolved_model tier < Opus, escalation section injected as '## Model Escalation Policy

{contents}

---

'
When resolved_model is opus or None, NO escalation section injected
When template file missing, returns None and prints warning to stderr
Escalation section appears BEFORE reorder instruction in prompt ordering
All TEST-INIT-003 tests pass
userStories.notes: Template is loaded fresh each iteration (not cached) to allow hot-editing. The model_tier() function from model.rs determines the tier. Inject position: after section 9 (non-code instruction) and before section 11 (reorder instruction) per PRD appendix ordering. Use eprintln! for the missing-file warning.

correctness: Template only injected for non-opus models
Section ordering matches PRD spec exactly
Missing file handled gracefully
performance: File read per iteration is acceptable — template file is small
style: Use std::fs::read_to_string with proper error handling
Warning to stderr, not stdout
Helper function is independently testable

userStories.title: Crash recovery model escalation fields (US-011)
userStories.description: Add last_task_id: Option<String> and last_was_crash: bool to IterationContext. At the end of each iteration (same location as ctx.last_files assignment ~line 413 of engine.rs), update these fields based on the iteration outcome. The actual escalation check is wired in FEAT-006.
acceptanceCriteria: IterationContext has last_task_id: Option<String> field, initialized to None
IterationContext has last_was_crash: bool field, initialized to false
After each iteration, last_task_id is set to the task_id that was worked on
After each iteration, last_was_crash is set based on whether the outcome was a crash
Fields are set at the same code location as ctx.last_files (~line 413)
All TEST-INIT-004 tests pass
userStories.notes: This task only adds the fields and updates them. The escalation CHECK (if same task + crash → call escalate_model) is done in FEAT-006 which orchestrates the full flow. Crash detection: match on IterationOutcome::Crash(CrashType) variant. last_was_crash is independent of CrashTracker backoff logic.

correctness: Must correctly identify crash outcomes vs non-crash outcomes
Fields must be set for every iteration, not just crashes
style: Set fields at same location as existing ctx.last_files for consistency

userStories.title: Engine orchestration — wire model through run_iteration (US-013)
userStories.description: Wire all model selection features together in run_iteration(). After build_prompt(), read resolved_model. Apply crash escalation if same task crashed. Pass effective model to spawn_claude() and print_iteration_header(). Log model to progress file.
acceptanceCriteria: After build_prompt(), effective_model = prompt_result.resolved_model
If ctx.last_task_id == Some(current_task_id) AND ctx.last_was_crash → effective_model = model::escalate_model(effective_model)
Escalation logged to stderr: 'Model escalated for crash recovery: {old} → {new}'
effective_model passed to spawn_claude() as model parameter
effective_model passed to print_iteration_header() as model parameter
Model name logged to progress file on each iteration
No change to IterationOutcome enum or return types
When effective_model is None, all downstream calls receive None (CLI default behavior)
log_iteration() signature in progress.rs updated to accept model: Option<&str> parameter
cargo test passes with no regressions
userStories.notes: This is the orchestration task that wires FEAT-001 through FEAT-005 together. Crash escalation timing per FR-009: AFTER build_prompt resolves base model, BEFORE spawn_claude. The log_iteration function in progress.rs needs a model parameter added. Import model::escalate_model and model::model_tier.

correctness: Crash escalation only when BOTH same task AND crash
Escalation timing: after build_prompt, before spawn_claude
None model passed through without escalation
performance: No extra DB queries — model comes from PromptResult
style: Use model:: prefix for imported functions
Log escalation clearly with old → new model names
Match existing engine.rs patterns for iteration orchestration

Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
No tight coupling between modules
Code is easy to change and extend
